summary: Food recipe app focused on Indian dishes, featuring video-based recipes with innovative UI/UX
architectural_notes: Indian cuisine as primary content focus
Emphasis on modern, innovative interface design

existing_features.title: Build a video-centric recipe browsing interface where each recipe is displayed as a large video card with overlay text showing the recipe name and key details
Implement an innovative grid or masonry layout for the recipe gallery with dynamic sizing, hover effects, and smooth transitions between views
Create a detailed recipe view that displays the full video prominently, along with ingredients list, cooking instructions, difficulty level, and cooking time in an aesthetically pleasing layout
Implement filtering and search functionality to browse recipes by cuisine type, difficulty level, cooking time, or recipe name

Design a cohesive visual theme inspired by Indian culture and culinary traditions, using warm, vibrant colors (such as saffron, turmeric gold, terracotta, deep red, and emerald green), elegant typography with modern Indian-inspired fonts, and thoughtful spacing that creates a premium, magazine-like feel
Render the generated hero banner and recipe category icons as static assets in frontend/public/assets/generated

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a data model to store recipe information including recipe name, description, cuisine type, difficulty level, cooking time, ingredients list, and video URL
Populate the app with at least 15 diverse Indian recipes covering various categories such as appetizers, main courses, breads, rice dishes, desserts, and beverages
Remove non-vegetarian dishes from the recipe collection
Remove all non-vegetarian recipes from the hardcoded recipe list in backend/main.mo, including Butter Chicken, Chicken Biryani, and any other meat-based dishes
Ensure the frontend recipe gallery and detail pages continue to display the filtered vegetarian-only recipe collection without any code changes
